topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the ballroom felt suddenly thin, suffocating. I watched him across the crowded floor, the way he laughed at something the groom said, and a tight, hard knot formed in my chest. Every polite nod I offered felt like a deliberate act of aggression. When our eyes finally met, the casual warmth vanished, replaced by a cold, sharp assessment. I gripped my champagne flute so tightly my knuckles bleached white. I didn't move to approach; instead, I turned my back abruptly, the sound of my silk dress rustling loud in the sudden silence of my own head. I needed distance. I needed to erase the last few years in one sharp, decisive motion.

[story 2]

"Oh, look who it is," a voice cut through my carefully constructed composure, dripping with saccharine sweetness. I felt a heat rising in my neck, a prickling sensation spreading across my skin that made my jaw clench involuntarily. He was standing too close, leaning in with an easy familiarity that felt like a physical violation. I forced a smile that felt brittle, a mask stretched thin over something raw and unsteady inside. My gaze flickered away, scanning the room as if searching for an escape route. I could feel the blood pounding behind my eyes, a restless energy demanding release. I took a long, slow sip of my drink, letting the silence between us become a heavy, unspoken accusation.

[story 3]

I saw him near the buffet table, and the world seemed to narrow down to that single point. A tremor ran through my hands, and I consciously kept my posture rigid, every muscle coiled tight. When he looked up and we connected, there was no softening in his expression, only a flicker of something guarded, something that mirrored the icy resolve I was cultivating. I stopped mid-conversation with my friend, the noise of the party receding into a dull hum. My breathing hitched, shallow and fast. I managed a curt nod, my voice sounding unnaturally flat as I walked toward the exit, each step deliberate, fueled by a simmering intensity I couldn't quite contain.
